L1_headline: + SEM – Q3 revenue up 7.2% YoY; Concentra fully separated. | 10/30/25
L2_brief: Select Medical Holdings filed its Q3 2025 10-Q reporting quarterly revenue of $1.36B (up 7.2% YoY) and net income of $44.2M from continuing operations. The company completed the spin-off of Concentra in Q4 2024, and now operates three segments: critical illness recovery hospitals, rehabilitation hospitals, and outpatient rehabilitation. No active M&A transactions disclosed, though the company maintains equity method investments including a recent hospital contribution to Banner JV.

L3_detailed.filing_purpose: Quarterly report for the period ended September 30, 2025, required periodic filing under Section 13 of the Securities Exchange Act.
key_information: Q3 2025 revenue of $1.363B vs $1.272B in Q3 2024 (7.2% increase)
Net income from continuing operations of $44.2M vs $41.3M prior year
Concentra distribution completed November 25, 2024 - now reported as discontinued operations for prior periods
Repurchased approximately 6.6 million shares YTD 2025 for approximately $100.4M
Medicare accounts receivable represents 21% of total receivables
financial_highlights: Total debt: $1.79B including $550M 6.25% senior notes and $1.04B term loan
Cash and equivalents: $60.1M as of September 30, 2025
L3_detailed.deal_relevance: No pending M&A transactions. Concentra separation completed in Q4 2024 with tax-free distribution of 104.1M shares. Company contributed a $45.8M hospital to Banner JV in September 2025 in exchange for equity interest. Transition services agreement with Concentra generates $9.9M YTD revenue, terminates by November 2026.
regulatory_mentions: DOJ/HHS investigation ongoing regarding physical therapy billing practices - qui tam lawsuit filed, DOJ declined to intervene but reserved rights
U.S. Attorney's Office for Western District of Oklahoma investigating SSH-Oklahoma City regarding respiratory therapy billing
Medicare represents significant portion of revenue - subject to CMS reimbursement rate changes
conditions_or_requirements: Interest rate cap limits exposure to 4.5% on $1.0B of term loan principal
Revolving credit facility capacity of $150M drawn as of quarter end
Self-insured retention limits on professional liability and general liability insurance programs
risks_flagged: DOJ investigation into physical therapy billing practices - False Claims Act qui tam lawsuit pending
Oklahoma City billing investigation ongoing - timing and outcome unpredictable
Concentration of Medicare receivables (21% of AR) creates credit and reimbursement risk
Healthcare regulatory environment - potential for additional investigations
Labor costs pressure - personnel expense remains largest cost component
